evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: In multiple urine samples, organic acid analysis revealed a prominent 2-EHA peak usually exceeding the size of the 2-MBG peak.

evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: Awareness of 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria as a diagnostic marker could lead to increased detection of SBCADD and improved definition of its clinical phenotype.

readouts.interpretation: Urinary 2-EHA reports increased R-pathway flux downstream of the S-pathway SBCAD block.
readouts.target: Metabolic rerouting via the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation

nodes.description: Urinary 2-ethylhydracrylic acid is a diagnostic organic acid marker in SBCADD, reflecting increased flux through the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.

evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: In multiple urine samples, organic acid analysis revealed a prominent 2-EHA peak usually exceeding the size of the 2-MBG peak.

nodes.description: The accumulating glycine conjugate 2-methylbutyrylglycine can increase lipid oxidation and reduce nonenzymatic antioxidant defenses in rat cerebral cortex preparations and C6 glioma cells, providing a plausible mechanism for neurologic vulnerability in the symptomatic minority.

evidence.reference_title: 2-Methylbutyrylglycine induces lipid oxidative damage and decreases the antioxidant defenses in rat brain.
evidence.snippet: 2MBG induced sulfhydryl oxidation in cortical supernatants and decreased glutathione (GSH) in these brain preparations, as well as in C6 cells

evidence.reference_title: 2-Methylbutyrylglycine induces lipid oxidative damage and decreases the antioxidant defenses in rat brain.
evidence.snippet: oxidative stress induced by 2MBG is involved, at least in part, in the pathophysiology of the brain damage found in SBCAD deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Increased urinary excretion of 2-methylbutyrylglycine (2MBG) is the hallmark of SBCAD deficiency.

readouts.interpretation: Urinary 2-MBG reports the isoleucine S-pathway block and upstream 2-methylbutyryl-CoA-derived metabolite accumulation.
readouts.target: Impaired isoleucine catabolism via SBCAD loss-of-function

nodes.description: Increased urinary excretion of 2-methylbutyrylglycine is the biochemical hallmark of SBCADD, detected by urine organic acid analysis.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Increased urinary excretion of 2-methylbutyrylglycine (2MBG) is the hallmark of SBCAD deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical, Clinical, and Genetic Characteristics of Short/Branched Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency in Chinese Patients by Newborn Screening.
evidence.snippet: All patients who underwent urinary organic acid analysis showed elevation of 2-methylburtyrylglycine in urine.

nodes.description: ACAD family enzymes display significant substrate promiscuity. In HEK-293 cell models, ACADSB knockout causes marked increases in C5-carnitine and substantial decreases in C3-carnitine, demonstrating that SBCAD activity influences short-chain acyl-CoA and acylcarnitine pools beyond a single metabolite. This promiscuity partially explains why complete SBCAD loss-of-function has limited clinical consequences in most individuals.
nodes.id: ACAD substrate promiscuity and compensatory enzymology

evidence.reference_title: Acyl-CoA dehydrogenase substrate promiscuity: Challenges and opportunities for development of substrate reduction therapy in disorders of valine and isoleucine metabolism.
evidence.snippet: Deficiencies of these acyl-CoA dehydrogenase (ACAD) enzymes are considered biochemical abnormalities with limited or no clinical consequences.

evidence.reference_title: ACADSB / 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: ACADSB | HGNC:91 | 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency | MONDO:0012392 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Biallelic ACADSB pathogenic variants reduce short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase catalytic activity in mitochondria.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency, also known as short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase (SBCAD) deficiency, is a recently described autosomal recessive disorder of L-isoleucine metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of new ACADSB gene sequence mutations and clinical implications in patients with 2-methylbutyrylglycinuria identified by newborn screening.
evidence.snippet: Short/branched chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase (SBCAD) deficiency, also known as 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency, is a recently described autosomal recessive disorder of isoleucine metabolism. Most patients reported thus far have originated from a founder mutation in the Hmong Chinese population.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn errors of isoleucine degradation: a review.
evidence.snippet: Confirmation of diagnosis is therefore advisable by specific enzyme assay and/or mutation analysis of the ACAT1 (beta-KT), ACADSB (SBCAD) or HADH2 (MHBD) genes.

nodes.description: Autism spectrum disorder has been reported in a minority of SBCADD patients on longitudinal follow-up.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: On longitudinal follow-up, epilepsy, developmental delay, microcephaly, and autism can develop.

evidence.reference_title: Acyl-CoA dehydrogenase substrate promiscuity: Challenges and opportunities for development of substrate reduction therapy in disorders of valine and isoleucine metabolism.
evidence.snippet: deletion of ACADSB in HEK-293 cells led to an equally strong decrease in C3-carnitine when compared to wild-type cells.

readouts.interpretation: Decreased C3-carnitine reports broader ACADSB-dependent acylcarnitine remodeling in knockout cells.

nodes.description: L-carnitine supplementation (typically 50-100 mg/kg/day) is used to support conjugation and excretion of accumulating acyl-CoA intermediates as acylcarnitine species. Carnitine supplementation is one of the most consistently recommended interventions in SBCADD management.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Carnitine supplementation and valproate avoidance appear to be indicated.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Longitudinal biochemical monitoring of the two patients while on treatment with carnitine (100 mg/kg/day) was provided.

nodes.description: Prevention of metabolic decompensation through avoidance of prolonged fasting, protein overload, and provision of an emergency protocol for management of intercurrent febrile illnesses and acute catabolic episodes.
nodes.id: Catabolic stress avoidance and emergency protocol

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Providing an emergency protocol for the management of acute catabolic episodes seems reasonable in asymptomatic patients with SBCAD deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Catabolic situations can precipitate acute metabolic decompensation.

nodes.description: Motor and cognitive developmental delay is reported in a minority of symptomatic SBCADD patients. One of the earliest reported patients had severe motor developmental delay with muscle atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed athetoid cerebral palsy, and another had severe motor developmental delay with muscle atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical onset occurs in newborns or later in life with seizures, developmental delay, hypotonia, and failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Dietary intervention in SBCADD is controversial. Early-identified cases historically received low-protein diets with fasting avoidance, but the indication for disruptive dietary manipulations in asymptomatic newborn-screening-identified infants is questionable. Current practice favors maintaining a normal diet with vigilance during intercurrent illness rather than protein restriction.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: Dietary treatment was initiated in the neonatal period. Except for 1 patient who developed mild muscle hypotonia, all patients remain asymptomatic at ages ranging from 3 to 14 months of age.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: The continued efficacy of long-term dietary therapy instituted presymptomatically remains to be established.

nodes.description: Athetoid cerebral palsy was reported in one of the original patients described with SBCADD.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed athetoid cerebral palsy, and another had severe motor developmental delay with muscle atrophy.

nodes.description: Elevated C5-acylcarnitine (2-methylbutyrylcarnitine, isobaric with isovalerylcarnitine) on tandem mass spectrometry newborn screening is the primary ascertainment marker for SBCADD.

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical, Clinical, and Genetic Characteristics of Short/Branched Chain Acyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency in Chinese Patients by Newborn Screening.
evidence.snippet: all patients showed slightly or moderately elevated C5-carnitine concentrations

nodes.description: Failure to thrive is reported in symptomatic SBCADD patients, likely related to feeding difficulties and recurrent metabolic instability.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical onset occurs in newborns or later in life with seizures, developmental delay, hypotonia, and failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Loss-of-function variants reduce SBCAD enzyme activity and stability, blocking this catabolic step and causing accumulation of upstream metabolites that are diverted to measurable conjugates including C5-acylcarnitine (blood) and 2-methylbutyrylglycine (urine).
nodes.id: Impaired isoleucine catabolism via SBCAD loss-of-function

evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: Isolated excretion of 2-methylbutyrylglycine (2-MBG) is the hallmark of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (SBCADD), a recently identified defect in the proximal pathway of L-isoleucine oxidation.

nodes.description: The majority of individuals with SBCADD remain clinically asymptomatic. In a literature review of 162 patients, SBCADD was symptomatic in only about 10% of reported patients.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Based on our experience and the literature review (162 patients), SBCAD deficiency is symptomatic in about 10% of reported patients.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of new ACADSB gene sequence mutations and clinical implications in patients with 2-methylbutyrylglycinuria identified by newborn screening.
evidence.snippet: Our patients have been well without treatment and call for careful follow-up studies to learn the true clinical impact of this disorder.

nodes.description: The metabolic block in the S-pathway of isoleucine oxidation leads to increased flux through the normally minor R-pathway, producing 2-ethylhydracrylic acid (2-EHA) as a prominent urinary marker. This R-pathway flux may function as a compensatory safety valve that mitigates severity in many individuals, explaining the largely benign phenotype observed in most SBCADD patients.
nodes.id: Metabolic rerouting via the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation

evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: Increased flux through the R-pathway may act as a safety valve for overflow of accumulating S-pathway metabolites and thereby mitigate the severity of SBCADD.

evidence.reference_title: 2-ethylhydracrylic aciduria in short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: application to diagnosis and implications for the R-pathway of isoleucine oxidation.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 40-46% of total 2-methylbutyric acid conjugates were in the form of the R-isomer, indicating significant metabolism via the R-pathway.

nodes.description: Microcephaly has been reported in a minority of SBCADD patients on longitudinal follow-up.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: On longitudinal follow-up, epilepsy, developmental delay, microcephaly, and autism can develop.

nodes.description: Severe motor developmental delay has been reported in symptomatic SBCADD.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed athetoid cerebral palsy, and another had severe motor developmental delay with muscle atrophy.

nodes.description: Multiple missense variants in ACADSB have been identified in non-Hmong patients through newborn screening. Expression studies show these variants lead to inactivation or instability of the mutant SBCAD enzymes. At least 15 pathogenic variants are known in ACADSB.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of new ACADSB gene sequence mutations and clinical implications in patients with 2-methylbutyrylglycinuria identified by newborn screening.
evidence.snippet: Escherichia coli expression studies revealed that the missense mutations identified lead to inactivation or instability of the mutant SBCAD enzymes.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is a recognized feature of symptomatic SBCADD, reported in both early-onset presentations and in newborn-screening-identified patients.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: Except for 1 patient who developed mild muscle hypotonia, all patients remain asymptomatic at ages ranging from 3 to 14 months of age.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical onset occurs in newborns or later in life with seizures, developmental delay, hypotonia, and failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Seizures are reported in symptomatic SBCADD patients, occurring at clinical onset or developing during follow-up. Epilepsy may develop on longitudinal follow-up.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Clinical onset occurs in newborns or later in life with seizures, developmental delay, hypotonia, and failure to thrive.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: On longitudinal follow-up, epilepsy, developmental delay, microcephaly, and autism can develop.

nodes.description: Muscle atrophy was reported in one of the original SBCADD patients, associated with severe motor developmental delay.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed athetoid cerebral palsy, and another had severe motor developmental delay with muscle atrophy.

nodes.description: Overlapping substrate specificity among ACAD family enzymes may compensate for SBCAD deficiency under normal conditions. During catabolic stress (infection, fasting), this compensatory capacity may be exceeded, leading to metabolic decompensation in a minority of patients. This stress-sensitive model explains why most patients remain asymptomatic but a small fraction develop clinical disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Catabolic situations can precipitate acute metabolic decompensation.

nodes.description: Valproate should be avoided in SBCADD patients because valproyl-CoA can serve as a substrate of SBCAD, and valproate administration may exacerbate the metabolic block.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and molecular spectrum of short/branched-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency: two new cases and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Carnitine supplementation and valproate avoidance appear to be indicated.

nodes.description: The c.1165A>G variant in ACADSB causes skipping of exon 10 and is a founder mutation highly prevalent in the Hmong population and in several Chinese ethnic minority groups (Miao, Dong). Homozygosity for this variant is found at a frequency of approximately 1.3% in the Hmong population, with a carrier frequency of 21.8%.

evidence.reference_title: Prospective diagnosis of 2-methylbutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency in the Hmong population by newborn screening using tandem mass spectrometry.
evidence.snippet: Molecular genetic analysis performed for 3 of these patients revealed that all are homozygous for an 1165A>G mutation that causes skipping of exon 10 of the SBCAD gene.
